title: Lifecycle Destructive Refusal Coverage
goal: Harden root lifecycle uninstall coverage so destructive operations refuse ambiguous ownership, preserve repo-owned and local-only state by default, and keep dry-run/apply planning aligned.
non_goals: Do not automatically delete ambiguous files.
Do not remove repo-owned content outside managed fences.
Do not redesign lifecycle module installers.

intent.outcome: Root lifecycle uninstall behavior is proven by black-box fixtures for ambiguous and safe managed-only states.
intent.constraints: Keep behavior conservative; destructive output must stay actionable and compact.
intent.latitude: Add focused tests and small lifecycle output/plan fixes if the tests reveal drift.
intent.escalation: Stop before changing package ownership semantics or broad installer internals.

execution.next_step: Archive this plan, commit the milestone, close #483, refresh external evidence, then continue #484.
execution.proof: Focused uninstall lifecycle tests, relevant package installer tests, full workspace CLI tests, ruff, proof selector, authority checks, planning checks, preflight, closeout trust, skills query, and reconcile ran.

invariants: Uninstall refuses ambiguous ownership unless explicitly safe.
Local-only state is preserved by default.
Dry-run and apply use the same destructive planner.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Make lifecycle management straightforward, predictable, and low-judgment.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Agents can trust uninstall refusal and preservation behavior from black-box fixtures.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Legacy compatibility machinery removal belongs to #484.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: Root uninstall refuses root shared-layer removals when any shared-layer payload is ambiguous, preserves local-only and repo-owned startup state by default, and has dry-run/apply agreement fixtures for safe managed-only removals.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Reingest and repeat until out of issues.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Continue the lifecycle hardening lane with #483 after #482 closed.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Add destructive uninstall/refusal coverage and fix narrow drift if found.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Verify safe managed-only uninstall, ambiguous refusal, local-only preservation, and dry-run/apply agreement.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Root lifecycle code/output/tests, module installer tests if needed, and planning surfaces.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: Focused pytest, relevant package installer pytest, ruff, proof selector, planning checks.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Stop before broad lifecycle primitive extraction or module installer rewrites.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Branch switching, unrelated planning maturity work, or generated package work.

stop_conditions.stop when: Acceptance fixtures pass and closeout proof is recorded.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: Safe uninstall requires changing ownership semantics.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: The work becomes #484 legacy removal/refusal policy.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: Dry-run/apply agreement cannot be shown without broader behavior changes.

context_budget.live working set: #483, root uninstall planner/output, workspace CLI tests, package installer tests, active execplan.
context_budget.recoverable later: New planning maturity lane and historical derived follow-up backlog.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update this execplan with proof and closeout.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: summary, report next_action, issue refresh, and skills query used.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Implement #483 black-box destructive lifecycle refusal coverage.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Before commit or if uninstall behavior requires package installer semantic changes.

delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Stay on current branch, commit after proof, preserve conservative destructive behavior.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Fixture arrangement, assertion shape, and minor output clarity fixes.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A change would delete ambiguous or repo-owned state automatically.

active_milestone.scope: Root lifecycle uninstall refusal and preservation coverage.
active_milestone.ready: Issue #483 is open and #482 is closed.

immediate_next_action: Archive this plan, commit the milestone, close #483, refresh external evidence, then promote #484.
invariants: Destructive uninstall must refuse ambiguous ownership.
Local-only state is preserved unless --local-only is selected.
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "uninstall_dry_run_requires_review or uninstall_apply_refuses or uninstall_dry_run_and_apply_agree or uninstall_invokes_selected_module"
uv run ruff check src tests packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py packages/memory/tests/test_installer.py

uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed src/agentic_workspace/cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/lifecycle-destructive-refusal-coverage.plan.json --format json
uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section root_cli_authority --format json
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --section workflow_obligations --format json
uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --section closeout_trust --format json
uv run agentic-workspace skills --target . --task "dogfooding closeout review" --format json
completion_criteria: Uninstall refuses ambiguous ownership with an actionable reason.
Local-only state is preserved by default.
Repo-owned startup files and unfenced content are preserved.
Dry-run and apply agree on planned destructive actions.
Tests cover at least one ambiguous ownership case and one safe managed-only uninstall.

execution_run.what happened: Added conservative root shared-layer uninstall refusal: if any root workspace payload differs from the managed payload, otherwise removable root shared-layer payloads are skipped until manual review. Added black-box root fixtures for ambiguous apply refusal, safe managed-only dry-run/apply agreement, local-only preservation, repo-owned AGENTS preservation, and selected module uninstall dispatch.
execution_run.scope touched: Root lifecycle uninstall runtime, workspace CLI tests, and checked-in planning surfaces.
execution_run.changed surfaces: preflight, summary, report, proof, reconcile, doctor, skills, root_cli_authority defaults, planning surface check.
execution_run.validations run: Focused uninstall lifecycle tests; planning package uninstall tests; memory package uninstall tests; full tests/test_workspace_cli.py; ruff; proof selector; root CLI authority; planning doctor; planning surface check; preflight; workflow_obligations and closeout_trust reports; reconcile; dogfooding skills query.
execution_run.result for continuation: #483 is ready to close; lifecycle-management-hardening continues with #484.
execution_run.next step: Archive plan, commit, push, close #483, refresh external evidence, then continue #484.

finished_run_review.scope respected: yes; changes stayed in root lifecycle uninstall behavior, workspace CLI tests, and planning state.
finished_run_review.intent served: yes; acceptance fixtures cover ambiguous ownership refusal, local-only preservation, repo-owned startup preservation, dry-run/apply agreement, and safe managed-only uninstall.
finished_run_review.config compliance: used package workflow surfaces and stayed on the current branch.
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: low; the behavior is conservative and only tightens destructive root shared-layer removal.
finished_run_review.follow-on decision: close #483 and continue lifecycle hardening with #484.

proof_report.validation proof: 204 workspace CLI tests passed; focused root uninstall tests passed; planning and memory package uninstall subsets passed; ruff passed; proof selector, root CLI authority, planning doctor, planning surface check, summary, preflight, workflow_obligations, closeout_trust, reconcile, and skills query completed.
proof_report.proof achieved now: Uninstall refuses ambiguous root shared-layer deletion and preserves local-only and repo-owned startup state by default.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: #483 Harden uninstall and destructive lifecycle refusal coverage.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: Root uninstall now skips otherwise removable shared-layer payloads when another shared-layer file is ambiguous; tests verify actionable review output, safe managed-only dry-run/apply agreement, local-only preservation, repo-owned AGENTS preservation, and module dispatch.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Make the package streamlined, lightweight, frictionless, cheap, modular, and small while preserving leverage.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Conservative runtime guard plus tests; no new top-level command or broad lifecycle redesign.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Does lifecycle uninstall reduce judgment without increasing first-contact burden?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: The bounded slice is complete and remaining intent is routed to a checked-in continuation owner.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Prepared closeout records intent satisfaction, closure decision, proof evidence, and distillation buckets.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen when .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml roadmap lane lifecycle-management-hardening activates a fresh bounded slice.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Root lifecycle uninstall is more conservative and better proven for destructive refusal and preservation behavior.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: Focused and full workspace CLI tests, package uninstall subsets, ruff, proof selector, root CLI authority, planning doctor, planning surface check, summary, preflight, closeout reports, reconcile, and skills query passed.

signals found: A plausible compact report selector, current_work, is rejected even though the concept appears in summary; route after this slice unless already covered.
